title: Rose Byrne Wins Golden Globe for Best Actress in a Comedy or Musical, Says Husband Bobby Cannavale Missed Show for a Reptile Expo
text: Rose Byrne claimed her first Golden Globe award for her role in the psychological drama 'If I Had Legs I'd Kick You,' where she portrays a mother experiencing a mental health crisis. The Australian actress expressed genuine surprise at winning, quipping about her dark film being nominated in the comedy category: 'I didn't sing in this movie. This is such a shock!' Byrne praised the film's writer-director Mary Bronstein and noted the impressive production efficiency, stating the movie was 'shot in 25 days for, like, $8.50.' She emphasized how significant this recognition was for such a modest independent production. The actress revealed an amusing detail during her acceptance speech: her brother served as her date because her longtime partner Bobby Cannavale was attending a reptile expo in New Jersey to prepare for acquiring a bearded dragon. Byrne defeated fellow nominees including Cynthia Erivo, Kate Hudson, Chase Infiniti, Amanda Seyfried, and Emma Stone. The win marks her first Golden Globe after receiving two previous nominations for the television series 'Damages.' The film, which premiered at Sundance, has garnered additional recognition including a Silver Bear at the Berlin Film Festival.
